nativeRange.habitat: Coral-rich areas of lagoons and seaward reefs, often near caves and ledges; depth range 0–110 m, commonly above 48 m

diet.notes: Specialised wild feeder on sponges and tunicates, which makes it notoriously difficult to convert to prepared foods. Aquarium diet should include sponge-based angelfish preparations, spirulina, marine algae, mysis shrimp, and finely chopped seafood, offered in frequent small meals.

avoidWith: highly aggressive fish that outcompete it for food

summary: A jewel-toned Indo-Pacific angelfish prized for its bold orange, blue and yellow banding; small for a large angel but notoriously delicate, with strong preference for sponge-based diets and clean, established reef systems.
careNotes: The Regal Angelfish is one of the most challenging marine angelfishes to keep, and is generally considered expert-only. In the wild it feeds heavily on sponges and tunicates, and many specimens never fully transition to prepared foods in captivity. Survivability is reported to vary by collection region — specimens from the Red Sea and Maldives tend to acclimate more readily than those from the broader Indo-Pacific (Philippines, Indonesia), which historically have very high mortality rates; selecting locality and choosing actively feeding fish at point of sale strongly improves outcomes. Generally advised to introduce the Regal Angelfish first into the aquarium so it can establish a territory before more assertive fish are added. Provide a mature system with extensive live rock for grazing, caves and ledges for retreat, and open swimming space; an active swimmer that uses the full water column. Reef compatibility is mixed — it is commonly considered "reef-safe with caution": it has been known to nip at large-polyped stony corals, some soft corals, and clam mantles, but is generally tolerant of small-polyped stony corals and noxious soft corals. Sources disagree slightly on minimum tank size (LiveAquaria 125 US gal / ~473 L; some retailers 100 US gal / ~380 L) — the larger end of this range, rounded up to 500 L, is used here given the species' active swimming behaviour and territorial needs. Temperature tolerance is broader in the wild (FishBase records 24.6–29 °C) than typical retailer recommendations (22–26 °C); the range 22–27 °C is used here to span both. Like other large angelfishes, the Regal is reported to be susceptible to head and lateral line erosion (HLLE) when kept in poor water quality or fed a diet lacking marine algae and vitamins.
breedingNotes: Has been reported to be a protogynous (female-first) sequential hermaphrodite, like other pomacanthids. Captive breeding is essentially undocumented in home aquaria; pair-bonded harems form on the reef but are not reliably reproduced in captivity.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Designated secondary (Reef2Reef wiki) returned 403; swapped to FishBase per playbook. Cross-checked taxonomy (family Pomacanthidae, order Acanthuriformes), maximum length (25 cm SL), native range (Red Sea, East Africa to Tuamoto Islands; north to Ryukyu/Ogasawara; south to GBR/New Caledonia), wild temperature range (24.6–29 °C), depth (0–110 m), maximum reported lifespan (15 years), and diet (sponges and tunicates).

additional.name: Aquarium Specialty
additional.notes: Additional husbandry cross-check after Reef2Reef wiki 403 and Reefs.com 403. Confirmed adult size ~25 cm (10 in), water parameters (72–78 °F / 22.2–25.6 °C, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025), captive lifespan 10–15 years. Reported minimum tank size 100 US gal (~380 L) — lower than LiveAquaria's 125 US gal (~473 L); larger end biased to 500 L per tank-size rule given the species' active swimming.

fish.breedingNotes: Protogynous hermaphrodite in the wild; captive breeding essentially undocumented in home aquaria.
